title: type CanAlloc: bool = true in Allocator trait should be const, not type
description: ferrum-lang-generics.md §1.1 Allocator trait shows: 'type CanAlloc: bool = true'. The 'type' keyword introduces an associated type (a type-level item). But 'bool' here is used as a bound on the type's kind (?) and 'true' is a value, not a type. This appears to be an associated constant: 'const CanAlloc: bool = true'. Using 'type' for this is inconsistent with the rest of the trait system where 'type' introduces associated types (like 'type Item' in Iterator) and values are introduced with 'const'. A caller implementing the Allocator trait will not know whether to write 'type CanAlloc = bool' (a type alias to bool) or 'const CanAlloc: bool = false' (a boolean constant). Fix: use 'const CanAlloc: bool = true' consistently with associated constants.
